footer.summary: Specialized in UX for tax, compliance, and financial platforms.

home.title: Designing consumer tax systems for globally mobile individuals navigating complex regulatory environments.
home.lead: I design identity-aware tax workflows that reduce compliance ambiguity and cognitive overload in high-stakes filing decisions.
home.systemsIntro: I work in structurally complex, policy-driven environments.
home.systems1Body: Designing logic-aware identity flows that determine eligibility, filing status, and regulatory branching.
home.systems2Body: Structuring policy-constrained decision trees where errors carry financial and legal consequences.
home.systems3Title: 3. Cognitive Load in High-Stakes Filing Flows
home.systems3Body: Reducing anxiety-driven abandonment in complex, multi-step regulatory journeys.
home.principlesIntro: I design systems through a structural lens, focusing on how early decisions shape downstream outcomes in complex, policy-driven environments.
home.principles1Body: I prioritize identity clarity before detailed data collection. Users should understand their position in a system before being asked to provide information.
home.principles1Bullet1: Prevents misclassification early in the flow
home.principles1Bullet3: Aligns user mental model with system logic
home.principles2Body: Regulatory rules are not backend constraints — they are part of the user experience.
home.principles2Bullet1: Surface implications at the right moment
home.principles3Body: I design not just for task completion, but for confident decision-making.
home.principles3Bullet3: Support user trust in high-stakes scenarios
home.principles4Title: Minimize Cognitive Load in Complex Flows
home.principles4Body: Complex systems should not feel complex to the user.
home.featuredCaseTitle: Reframing Identity Entry in Consumer Tax Systems
home.featuredCaseBody: Observed systemic friction in identity classification and regulatory branching for globally mobile filers.
home.project1Body: Designing decision systems under uncertainty in financial environments.
home.project2Body: Structuring contractor-client matching and conversion workflows.
home.project3Body: Optimizing trust-building and conversion in high-cost residential service journeys.
home.approachTitle: From inside the tax workflow, where work is often misclassified, misrouted, and difficult to locate across the system.
home.approach1Body: The friction a tax pro feels in WorkCenter, with a client waiting and documents already on the table, is usually a symptom of a decision made earlier in the system. The real breakdown often happens at booking, classification, or routing. I work backward from that moment to understand where the system first misunderstood the work.
home.approach2Title: Designing for Roles, Not Just Screens
home.approach2Body: Tax prep moves across associates, senior pros, clients, certification limits, and office-level availability. A clean UI does not help if the case lands on the wrong person, or if locating the right tax pro requires manually checking across multiple offices while a client is waiting on the phone. I design for role boundaries, escalation paths, and handoffs as deliberately as I design for individual screens.
home.approach3Body: The work is not simply about collecting more information. It is about collecting the right information at the right moment with the least possible burden. In tax workflows, accuracy competes with cognitive load, conversion competes with downstream operational cost, and what feels simple at booking can become expensive inside the office.
home.approach4Body: A document upload is not the same as case understanding. A shorter flow can hide the exact details that determine compliance, certification, or routing. From working directly with clients, I learned that the real goal is not for the system to simply have information, but for the right information to appear at the right moment for the right role.
home.aboutBody: UX Designer specializing in regulatory and financial systems design.

about.title: About
about.intro: I design systems for complex, policy-driven environments where decisions carry real financial and regulatory consequences. My work focuses on making ambiguity visible, structuring decision flows, and supporting confident actions in high-stakes contexts.
about.backgroundBody1: I come from a dual background in tax operations and UX design, working directly within real-world filing environments while studying interaction and product systems.
about.backgroundBody2: At H&R Block, I worked across client-facing tax preparation, where I observed how classification, routing, and regulatory logic impact both the user experience and operational efficiency. This experience shaped how I approach design today, not as isolated screens, but as interconnected systems involving people, rules, and decisions.
